videos.title: How To Kill A Volcano
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Volcanoes are one of the most powerful forces on Earth; their lava can destroy cities and their ash clouds can blot out the sun. And there is basically nothing we can do to make sure a volcano will never erupt again – except wait for it to die. So while we wait, what is it, exactly, that kills a vol

videos.title: The Weird Reason Rabies Is So Deadly
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
Rabies is the one of the deadliest diseases we know of; if you don’t get treatment soon after getting exposed, you’re almost certain to die. But rabies’ intent isn’t actually to kill – it's to control.

videos.title: How do Sperm Whales catch Giant Squid?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Sperm whales catch giant squid with a mechanism called 'suction feeding' - it sounds really strange, but it makes sense when you consider they are underwater. It's not too different from you getting the bubbles from your boba with a straw.

videos.title: Why Don't Blue Whales Eat Fish?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
The most massive land animals in the world all eat little stuff: grass and leaves. And almost all of the most massive aquatic animals also eat something itty bitty: tiny crustaceans called krill. Why do such big things eat such small things?

videos.description: You've probably heard that bees use a complex series of movements – often called the "waggle dance" – to tell other worker bees where to find new sources of nectar…it’s a super clever and sophisticated system. But it turns out that bees almost never immediately jump up to follow the directions they

videos.title: There’s Really Only One Way To Die
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
Even though there are a lot of causes of death out there, they all actually boil down to one thing.

AI is supposed to make our lives easier, not harder. But IF it were to destroy the world, how would it do this?

videos.title: Weavers helped us get to the moon!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: The strongest collaborations are the most unusual ones! Expert weavers helped get us to the moon by enabling NASA to shrink the size of the Apollo Guidance computer so that it could actually be launched into space.

videos.title: The danger isn't over when the storm dies down.
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: The death toll of a natural disaster often occurs in the aftermath. For monkey island, the 'aftermath number' was 25. But the average hurricane has an aftermath number over 200.

videos.title: The Coyote Paradox
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Hunting coyotes can lead to MORE coyotes. If we want to control the coyote population, we will need to look at other approaches.

videos.title: Where do we get snake antivenom?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Snake antivenom is really difficult and expensive to produce, so it's not always available to those who need it. However, scientists are working on ways to make it cheaper and more accessible.

videos.title: You are not genetically related to all your ancestors.
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
It seems like a paradox, but you are not related to most of your ancestors. 

You inherited an equal number of genes from each of your parents, who received an equal number from their ancestors. There is a good chance you don't actually have any genes from most of your ancestors.

videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
The greater bulldog bat's call is as loud as a Jet Engine - but we cannot hear it. Its call is too high for our ears to pick up. 

We can't help but wonder what the world would sound like if we had a different range of hearing! Maybe we would be overwhelmed by the loud bats.

videos.title: Would you eat Mammoth Meat?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
Some mammoths have been found in Siberian permafrost - nicely preserved freezer-fresh meat. And recently some scientists used Mammoth DNA to clone cells, which is technically edible. But try to explain that allergic reaction to the ER...
Made with our friends at @CBSUnstoppable

videos.title: We need to think about efficiency differently!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: When we think about keeping our houses warm, we might want to optimize our heating - but there are many more ways to make sure your house stays warm and toasty! If we think about efficiency in a different way, we can use a lot less energy to reach the same goals.

videos.title: These planets aren't real!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
Have you heard of the planet Vulcan? No, not the version from Star Trek! Vulcan was a proposed planet that would orbit between Mercury and the Sun - speculations date back to the 17th century! 
Sadly, Vulcan stayed fictional.

videos.title: What makes a sound scary?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
What's the recipe for goosebumps and shivers? 
Let's talk about scary sounds! They are dissonant, shrill, and don't fit the setting you are in. Sometimes even cute sounds are scary when it's in the wrong setting. 
Made with our #stembesties at @CBSUnstoppable

videos.title: Could AI replace scientists?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: AI is very good at logic-based tasks, but doesn't make the same creative leaps that humans can make. AI can assist scientists, but not replace them.

videos.title: Your microbes give you away!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
You are covered with microorganisms. Everyone is! 
But everyone's microbe-cloud is different enough that scientists can pick out who has been in a room based only on the microorganisms they left behind!

videos.title: Why does boiled water freeze faster?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
We still don't know why recently boiled water freezes faster! 
A Tanzanian teen named Eraste Bartholomeo Mpemba was doing ice-cream experiments (yum!) when he found that recently boiled milk froze faster than room temperature milk - and he wondered why!

videos.title: How AI can assist scientists!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: It's become easier than ever to generate massive datasets - but dealing with all that information is challenging! AI, or Machine Learning, can help scientists analyse all that data and free up time to think about the results and do actual science.

videos.description:

videos.title: How worms wormed their way into American soil!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Earth worms are an invasive species and changed the American landscape!

videos.title: No thank you Nosferatu!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: The worst thing about being a vampire would be the diet. Blood is really not that nutritious and you'd run into several problems while maintaining your bloody diet!

videos.title: How do fish get into isolated mountain lakes?
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description:
Get 10% off 6" and 8.5" globes using the code "MINUTEEARTH" at https://www.movaglobes.com

The various ways new fish get into isolated lakes are a microcosm of the complicated ways that life has spread around the globe.

videos.title: Size matters for Hermit Crabs!
videos.author: MinuteEarth
videos.description: Hermit crabs grow throughout their life, but their shell doesn't! This is why they are always on the lookout for a new shell that fits perfectly.
